skill.name: business-contact-social-links-skill
skill.description: Automatically extract official websites and social media profiles from company names or website URLs. Supports LinkedIn, Facebook, X/Twitter, Instagram, YouTube, and TikTok discovery for lead generation, competitor analysis, and CRM enrichment.
skill.summary: Extract business websites and social media profiles from company names or URLs for lead generation and outreach.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 50 patterns across 229 lines including network requests, environment variable access, external commands, and a critical heuristic for combined code execution + network + credential access. After semantic evaluation, all findings are false positives: the Python script legitimately calls the BrowserAct API using a documented API key from environment variables, all hardcoded URLs point to the same trusted browseract.com domain, SKILL.md contains only documentation and shell usage examples (not executable code), and there is no evidence of data exfiltration, obfuscation, or malicious intent. The skill transparently performs its documented lead generation function. No prompt injection detected in skill files.

low_findings.title: Third-Party API Key Required for Operation
low_findings.description: The skill requires a BrowserAct API key (BROWSERACT_API_KEY) accessed via os.getenv(). The key is used to authenticate HTTP requests to the BrowserAct API service. This is documented and expected behavior, but users must create a paid BrowserAct account to use this skill.

low_findings.title: External API Network Requests
low_findings.description: The script makes HTTP POST and GET requests to api.browseract.com for task execution, status polling, and result retrieval. All requests target the same legitimate BrowserAct API domain. User-provided company names and URLs are sent to this service as input parameters.

low_findings.title: Shell Command Invocation Pattern
low_findings.description: The SKILL.md instructs agents to invoke the Python script via shell commands (e.g. python -u ./scripts/business_contact_social_links.py). This is the standard invocation pattern for skills with executable scripts and the command arguments are well-documented.

dangerous_patterns.title: Combined Network, Credential, and Command Execution Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer flagged the combination of network requests (HTTP to BrowserAct API), credential access (BROWSERACT_API_KEY from environment), and command execution (shell invocation of Python script) as a critical heuristic. After evaluation, this is a legitimate pattern: all three elements are necessary for the skill's documented functionality as a BrowserAct API client wrapper.

content.user_title: Extract Business Social Media Links and Contacts
content.value_statement: Finding official websites and social media profiles for businesses is time-consuming manual work. This skill automates the process by searching company names or scraping websites to extract verified social media links and contact information.
actual_capabilities: Find official website and social media profiles from a company name
Extract social media links including LinkedIn, X, Facebook, Instagram, YouTube, and TikTok from any website
Auto-detect whether input is a company name or website URL and route to the correct extraction method
Return structured results with platform names and canonical profile URLs
Poll long-running browser automation tasks with timestamped progress updates
Handle API authentication errors with clear user guidance instead of silent failures
limitations: Requires a paid BrowserAct API key to function
Task execution may take several minutes for complex websites with many pages
Cannot bypass websites that actively block automated access or scraping
Limited to social media platforms supported by the BrowserAct extraction templates

use_cases.title: Sales Lead Enrichment
use_cases.description: Find official websites and social media channels for a list of company names to build complete prospect profiles before outreach.

use_cases.title: Competitor Social Media Analysis
use_cases.description: Discover which social media platforms competitors prioritize by extracting all linked profiles from their websites.

use_cases.title: CRM Data Cleaning and Verification
use_cases.description: Verify and populate missing social links and official URLs for existing leads in a CRM database.

prompt_templates.title: Find Company Social Links
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the official website and social media profiles for the company Tesla.
prompt_templates.scenario: Basic company name search for a single well-known brand

prompt_templates.title: Scrape Website for Social Links
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract all social media profile links from https://www.stripe.com.
prompt_templates.scenario: Direct website URL scraping to discover linked social profiles

prompt_templates.title: Batch Process Multiple Companies
prompt_templates.prompt: Find social media links and official websites for these companies: Salesforce, HubSpot, Zendesk, Intercom, Freshworks.
prompt_templates.scenario: Multi-company lead generation for a list of target accounts

prompt_templates.title: Competitor Digital Footprint Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: For each of my top competitors (I will provide the list), extract all social media profiles from their websites and identify which platforms they are most active on.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced competitive intelligence gathering with comparative analysis

output_examples.input: Find social media profiles for OpenAI
output_examples.output:
Company Name: OpenAI
Current Page URL: https://openai.com
Social Media Information:
- X (Twitter): https://twitter.com/OpenAI
- LinkedIn: https://linkedin.com/company/openai
- YouTube: https://youtube.com/@OpenAI
- Instagram: https://instagram.com/openai

Found 4 social media profiles from figma.com:
- platform: X (Twitter), url: https://twitter.com/figma
- platform: LinkedIn, url: https://linkedin.com/company/figma
- platform: YouTube, url: https://youtube.com/@Figma
- platform: Instagram, url: https://instagram.com/figma

Company Name: Patagonia, Inc.
Current Page URL: https://www.patagonia.com
Social Media Information:
- Facebook: https://facebook.com/Patagonia
- X (Twitter): https://twitter.com/patagonia
- Instagram: https://instagram.com/patagonia
- YouTube: https://youtube.com/@patagonia
- LinkedIn: https://linkedin.com/company/patagonia

best_practices: Set the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable before invoking the skill to avoid authentication errors
Provide full official company names for more accurate Google-based search results
Monitor the timestamped status output while waiting for long-running tasks to complete
anti_patterns: Do not retry more than once if the task fails without a clear error message from the API
Do not use partial or abbreviated company names that may match multiple unrelated businesses
Do not paste the API key directly into the command instead of setting it as an environment variable

faq.question: What is BrowserAct and why do I need an API key?
faq.answer: BrowserAct is a browser automation service that handles web scraping and data extraction. An API key is required to authenticate your requests to their service.

faq.question: How long does a typical search take to complete?
faq.answer: Most tasks complete within 1 to 3 minutes. The script polls every 10 seconds and shows timestamped status updates while waiting for results.

faq.question: Can I search for multiple companies at the same time?
faq.answer: The script processes one target at a time. Run it multiple times with different company names or website URLs for batch processing.

faq.question: Which social media platforms can the skill detect?
faq.answer: The skill can extract LinkedIn, Facebook, X (Twitter), Instagram, YouTube, and TikTok profile links from search results and website pages.

faq.question: What should I do if I get an authorization error?
faq.answer: Verify your BrowserAct API key is valid and correctly set in the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable. Visit the BrowserAct Console to check or regenerate your key.

faq.question: Where is my company data sent during a search?
faq.answer: Company names and website URLs are sent to the BrowserAct API for processing. No data is stored or shared beyond what is needed to complete your search request.
